feed.title: AO3 - Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Evan "Buck" Buckley/Eddie Diaz (9-1-1 TV) on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: The One That Got Away
entry.summary:
Author: messy_gay_guy

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,716Chapters: 3/3Kudos: 9

Summary: Eddie Diaz has always been good at surviving. He survived the war. He survived Shannon. He survived a bullet, a well, and a thousand near-misses in between. He even survived realizing (too late, always too late) that what he felt for Buck was the kind of love you only recognize when it's already walking out the door. Now Buck is gone. And Eddie is learning, slowly and badly, that surviving and living are not the same thing. He tends an altar in the corner of his living room. He buys flowers every week. He dances alone. And somewhere, from wherever the dead go when they love you enough to stay close... Buck is watching.

  • Major Character Death
  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.summary:
Author: lafdbuckley

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 4,321Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 0

entry.title: the rest was just catching up
entry.summary:
Author: stillmercy

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,273Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 313

Summary: “I heard you two were expanding your family! Another little one at Durand. We were so excited when we saw the enrollment paperwork.” She clasps her hands together. “Theo is an absolute joy. Let me show you what he’s been working on.” Expanding your family. Buck opens his mouth to redirect — it's a reflex, the correction sitting right there on his tongue. This isn’t the first time they’ve been mistaken for more than two totally platonic best friends who are both men and also raising children together. It’s right there, polite and practiced: Oh, we’re not actually— But suddenly Eddie’s hand is on his back. Not his shoulder this time, but lower. A few inches lower than two totally platonic best friends who are both men and also raising children together would justify. Eddie’s palm presses flat against the base of Buck’s spine, fingers spread wide like he’s anchoring them both to the same spot. “Thank you so much,” Eddie says. “We’re really proud of him.” Or: The totally platonic act of raising not just one, but two kids with your straight best friend.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: The Valentine's Day Curse
entry.summary:
Author: rezkat03

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,601Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 12

Summary: Buck makes it his goal to have a lovely evening on his first date-date with Eddie. The only problem is, their first date falls on Valentine's Day.... And Buck will do everything in his power to avoid the cursed day from ruining his dinner.

entry.title: We’ll turn in to daisies, yeah, we’ll turn into stars
entry.summary:
Author: Naterboo

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,187Chapters: 1/8Kudos: 10

Summary: He’s still drunk—that much is clear when he smiles crookedly at Eddie, watching him from beneath his lashes. Eddie turns his face up from his phone and the neon lights paint his face in greens and blues, those dark eyes of his catching reflections in a way that makes them look almost ethereal.  Buck wants to— he wants to— His fingers tense against the brick at his back. Oh, it doesn’t matter. None of it really matters, because Eddie smiles at him. It dimples his cheeks and turns his eyes into half-moons. Buck’s own mouth lifts in a helpless mirror of it, joy blooming in his chest. Eddie closes the distance between them in a few casual strides, one hand hooked in his pocket and the other holding his phone to track their Uber. He stops just short of Buck’s feet. It’s not close enough, but at least Buck can feel the heat of him now.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: 'Cause You Love Somebody
entry.summary:
Author: love_me_anyway_118

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 1,370Chapters: 2/2Kudos: 27

Summary: Buck would do anything for Theo, especially adopting him. It wasn't an easy path but Buck was willing. The problem was that Theo had been thorough a lot in a short amount of time and he needed stability in his life right now, which meant two parents and preferably a kid. That wasn't Bucks strong suit.But it was Eddie's.

entry.title: let's talk about all the things that keep us up all night
entry.summary:
Author: martianbarnes

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,505Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 100

Summary: He presses the red button, puts the phone away to the side and reaches into the sink to pick up the last cup to clean.A moment later the cup falls with a clatter from Buck's hand back into the sink.Buck turns his head to glance at his phone. The screen is dark and it looks completely non-threatening but Buck’s heart picks up its speed anyway.Did he just say “love you, bye” before sending a kiss to Eddie before hanging up?Carefully, he reaches and picks up his phone, but he doesn't unlock it, just stares at it for a long moment. Yes, he is pretty sure he did just that. Why on earth would he do that?

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: the red means I love you
entry.summary:
Author: lintcollector

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,868Chapters: 2/5Kudos: 25

Summary: A phone buzzed. It certainly wasn't Buck's, tucked in his uniform pants pocket, which meant... yep, Eddie had left his phone in here. The phone vibrated again, which allowed him to casually happen to glance over at the notification banner. TableSaver: Thank you for using TableSaver! El Gañoso — Table for 2 on... A reservation already? That was fast, almost as if Bobby himself was eager for Eddie to go on this... well, it was probably a date, given the whole romantic candlelight dinner at a high end establishment. Nausea crashed into Buck with the force of a tsunami. — Or: Buck finds out Eddie has an upcoming date, and consequently spirals. There's also a lot of cooking involved.

entry.title: I think I found a fear of mine
entry.summary:
Author: avidreader0202

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 9,294Chapters: 1/1Kudos: 49

Summary: Buck shifted like he was about to stand—like he was about to leave—and something primal and terrified ripped straight through Eddie’s chest.“No,” Eddie breathed, sharper than he meant to, his hand shooting out and catching Buck’s wrist with more desperation than strength. “Don’t—don’t go. Don’t leave me.”------Eddie’s greatest fear is dying alone — a reality he’s brushed up against more times than he cares to count. His second greatest fear is telling his best friend that he loves him. When a building collapse traps him beneath the wreckage, he’s forced to confront both fears at once.

entry.title: So I'll lay in your arms and pretend that it's love
entry.summary:
Author: MyArtificialFlowers

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 29,818Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 247

Summary: Buck and Tommy haven’t even been together for two months when Buck starts thinking about ending things. He hates himself for it, a little. He can’t put his finger on why the thought crosses his mind as he lays with his head on Tommy’s lap while they watch a documentary about a Napali mountaineer’s quest to summit the 14 peaks. It might have something to do with the put upon sigh Tommy had let out before agreeing to watch it with him. Or perhaps it was the fact that his fingers didn’t end up carding through Buck’s hair until he asked him to do it.  Buck knows that he could talk to someone about it. He has plenty of people who would be willing to listen but each time he goes to say something he remembers how proud they were of him when they found out about Tommy. They’re happy for him and already have so many other things to be upset about. He’s made them worry about him too many times already; it’s better if they think he’s finally okay.

entry.title: Monsoon Season
entry.summary:
Author: pinkpanther1970

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,468Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 5

Summary: "Eddie thinks about Pangea, the slow inevitable drift as tectonic plates shifted and new continents emerged. It’s absurd. People are not land masses. They don’t just drift apart like continents or icebergs in the ocean. There was nothing fateful in Eddie and Buck’s dissolve, nothing to do with destiny or the universe. It was simple manmade destruction. Did he stop picking up the phone? Or did Buck stop calling? It was Eddie who stopped responding to texts. There’s evidence, a whole damning record he can scroll through. Maybe if he were brave enough, he’d be able to work it out. Pick apart their conversations, find the cracks, identify the point where they started slipping away from each other. Eddie is not brave enough."   or the Diazes never move back to LA, Eddie lives his best gay life in El Paso, and Buck refuses to stop haunting the narrative

entry.title: Nine Years of Almost (And The Year it Finally Stuck)
entry.summary:
Author: Writeninembers

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 38,783Chapters: 7/11Kudos: 121

Summary: It starts as a mistake. A joke. Something that doesn’t mean anything. Eddie’s birthday ends.A midnight kiss.Buck’s birthday begins. They do it every year.Over.And over.And over. For nine years.Every year.The same person. Some years are easy.Some are messy.And some… shouldn’t have happened.

  • Anal Sex

entry.title: Second Chances
entry.summary:
Author: starylighting

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 929,836Chapters: 93/?Kudos: 130

Summary: Bobby and Athena are just two people trying to put their lives back together while figuring out how to co-parent. What starts as a complicated plan to foster kids in need slowly turns into something much deeper. Through the ups and downs of heartbreak, healing, and raising children who’ve been through so much, they find strength in each other. Over time, their partnership grows into love, and together, they build a story about family, trust, and second chances.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

  • Evan "Buck" Buckley & Athena Grant
  • Henrietta "Hen" Wilson/Karen Wilson

entry.title: Evergrowing
entry.summary:
Author: starylighting

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 683,397Chapters: 86/?Kudos: 35

Summary: Athena and Bobby are learning that parenting doesn’t end when the kids turn eighteen. With three grown children and two still at home, Bobby and Athena find themselves navigating new challenges at every stage of life. Each child carries their own struggles, some spoken and some hidden, but together the family works through the ups and downs with love, patience, and resilience. At the heart of it all, they’re reminded that family is a bond that grows, shifts, and deepens, no matter how old the kids get.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply
